mnemonics: CAP Theorem: Pick any two from Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance (like choosing two toppings from C, A, P).
Think of VM Sprawl as a 'Virtual Mess' growing out of control.
Remember SOA (Service-Oriented Architecture) as 'Services on Arrival' - ready-to-use components.
keyPoints: Distributed Systems connect autonomous computers to appear as a single coherent system.
Virtualization is a key enabling technology that allows for resource pooling and multi-tenancy.
The CAP Theorem states a distributed data store can't simultaneously guarantee Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance.
commonMistakes: Assuming distributed systems are the same as parallel systems (they focus on transparency over pure speed).
Believing cloud computing eliminates all scalability concerns (improper architecture can still lead to bottlenecks).
Confusing virtualization with cloud computing (virtualization is a technology that enables the cloud model).
